summary: Tested iteration-3: nearby-provider matching (2.5km radius) replacing random provider spawn. Added new pytest suite /app/backend/tests/test_provider_matching.py with 7 tests — all pass. Full regression: 37/37 backend tests pass (iter1 19/19 + iter2 11/11 + iter3 7/7). Frontend availability banner on Home page verified: renders '6 pros nearby · arriving in ~1 min' against Mysuru centre when pool is free; assertion confirmed via Playwright. The 'No pros nearby' branch is covered by backend 503 tests (direct UI toggle needs geolocation override — not tested via FE, but banner template handles both branches in code).
action_items: (Optional) Add a data-testid='availability-banner-count' span for easier exact-value assertions from automation.
(Optional) After the user grants geolocation, pass real browser coords to checkAvailability so the banner reflects the actual address, not just Mysuru centre.
(Optional) When availability=false on Home, also disable/visually dim the service cards (currently they still appear clickable and the 503 only surfaces after booking).
critical_code_review_comments: Race-condition retry path in create_booking (lines 441-456) does not duplicate the initial CAS; the fallback uses a plain `$set` without the `active_booking_id: None` guard. Under very high concurrency a third-party could grab the retry provider in the microsecond between _find_nearest_provider and update_one. Acceptable given current load, but flag for a second CAS.
cancel_booking calls _release_provider without checking that the booking status was NOT already 'cancelled'; double-cancel is already blocked by the CANCELLABLE_STATUSES check (returns 400) — safe.

reschedule_booking does NOT re-match provider when the slot changes; the same provider remains reserved across the reschedule, which is fine semantically but means /availability count won't be restored if the new slot is far out. Current product spec.

_seed_providers is idempotent (only seeds if collection empty) — good. But if the collection is partially populated (e.g. migration added 2 new names later), this check skips seeding entirely. Consider seeding by unique name upsert.
availability endpoint iterates all free providers in Python and sorts in-memory; fine for 8 providers, but would need a geospatial index (2dsphere) for any real scale.
SERVICE_RADIUS_KM (2.5) and CITY_AVG_SPEED_KMH (15) are hard-coded constants. Consider moving to env or per-city config for rollout to new cities.

tracking endpoint advances the live provider.lat/lng on the booking doc each poll but never syncs that drift back to db.providers.base_lat — so the provider's home base remains correct for matching. Good separation.
Availability response names field returns ALL nearby pros via 'providers'[:5] but count uses full list length — consistent, good.
test_credentials: Phone: any random 10-digit (tests use 9xxxxxxxxx). Demo OTP returned in /api/auth/request-otp response.
seed_data_creation: Tests use per-run random phone numbers (TEST_* names). Provider collection is reset (active_booking_id=None for all) via a module-scoped autouse fixture at module start AND teardown, so the suite is hermetic and doesn't depend on prior iteration cleanup. After all backend tests run, I manually reset providers again so downstream FE checks and main-agent self-test start clean with count=6.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration-3 suite is /app/backend/tests/test_provider_matching.py (7 tests: availability in/out of range, booking in-range, booking out-of-range 503, provider reserve→release on cancel, out-of-radius pros never assigned, tracking converges). The suite includes a module-scoped autouse fixture that resets db.providers.active_booking_id to None on entry and exit — so you can re-run safely even if older tests left reservations. If you see fewer than 6 pros in /api/availability for Mysuru centre, run: python -c "import asyncio,os; from motor.motor_asyncio import AsyncIOMotorClient; c=AsyncIOMotorClient(os.environ['MONGO_URL']); asyncio.run(c['\${DB_NAME}'].providers.update_many({},{'\$set':{'active_booking_id':None}}))" (load .env first). Service radius is 2.5km; pool size is 8 (6 in-range, 2 out-of-range named 'Manoj T.' and 'Devi S.'). Home.jsx checks availability against Mysuru centre (DEFAULT_LOC=12.2958,76.6394) — banner data-testid='availability-banner'.
rca of the issue: N/A — all requested behaviours pass. Random-provider regression is now fully replaced by a deterministic haversine match + CAS reservation; out-of-radius pros provably never assigned; 503 returned cleanly when pool empty or address outside 2.5km.
